name: Bourbeuse River
description: The slowest large stream in the Ozarks and one of its crookedest: from its head of navigation at Mint Spring the Bourbeuse runs ~116 river-miles in under 38 airline miles to the Meramec. Runoff-fed and murky (its watershed is largely cleared farmland over Pennsylvanian sandstone), with an inconsistent water supply — the upper half is seldom floatable without dragging in a dry summer. Not popular with the rental-canoe crowd (one small outfitter on the whole river); mostly local anglers. Catfish throughout; smallmouth in the upper reaches, spotted bass increasingly dominant below Noser Mill.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (state-managed accesses); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River near High Gate, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified. At the Hwy B bridge, several miles above the head of navigation at Mint Spring. Agnew: usefulness is QUESTIONABLE for much of the upper river because many tributaries add considerable water after rain — if it has rained, cross-check the Union gauge for a rise. Reads the upper reach.

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River at Union, MO
gauges.knownBias: CANDIDATE, NOT primary-verified. At the Highway 50 bridge at Union. Agnew: fairly reliable for the entire lower river EXCEPT the Noser Mill→Spring Creek stretch, which runs lower than the rest. Long record (to 1897).

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River above Union, MO
gauges.knownBias: VERIFIED but NOT real-time: USGS site service shows water-quality only, no uv 00060/00065 — discontinued for flow. Do NOT poll. Reference only.

gauges.name: Bourbeuse River near Owensville, MO
gauges.knownBias: VERIFIED but NOT real-time: only field visits 1943–2006, no uv. Discontinued. Do NOT poll. Reference only.

sections.name: Upper Bourbeuse (Mint Spring to Noser Mill)
sections.description: The upper half from the head of navigation at Mint Spring to Noser Mill (~59 river-miles). Very inconsistent flow — seldom floatable without dragging in a dry summer; anglers walk riffles to reach the big pools. Gradient 2.6 ft/mi. Public access is scarce, so visiting paddlers often face multi-day floats between usable accesses.

takeOut.name: Hwy 185 at Noser Mill (Devils Back Campground)

representativeGauge.rationale: Agnew keys the upper-river navigability table to the Bourbeuse River near High Gate gauge (Hwy B bridge)
representativeGauge.knownBias: questionable for much of the upper river — tributaries add water after rain; cross-check Union for rises

thresholds.source: Al Agnew, 'Bourbeuse River Overview', OzarkAnglers (2017-08-01), upper-reach navigability table @ High Gate gauge: 'Less than 10 cfs—the whole upper river will require walking many riffles.'
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '10-30 cfs—low but floatable with a lot of scraping bottom, especially in upper portions.'
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '30-60 cfs—easily floatable in lower portions, some scraping bottom likely in upper portions.'
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: top of 'easily floatable' band (30-60 cfs); 60-120 is 'high but floatable'.

Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '60-120 cfs—high but floatable'; '120-200 cfs—very high, muddy water likely.' (confidence HIGH: Agnew's published navigability table is the authoritative Missouri float reference — owner-approved 2026-07-06.)
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: 'Over 200 cfs—Probably too high.' (confidence HIGH: authoritative Agnew navigability table — owner-approved 2026-07-06.)

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: Below ~10 cfs at High Gate the whole upper river requires walking many riffles; 10–30 cfs is low but floatable with a lot of bottom-scraping. In a dry summer (Jun–Nov normal 2–20 cfs) the upper river is frequently unfloatable.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Runoff-fed and flashy from tributaries; over ~120 cfs it is high and muddy, over ~200 cfs probably too high. Low-water bridges (Glaser Ford, Hog Trough Rd) and logjams are the practical hazards. NWS HTGM7 flood/action stages auto-fill at ingest.
characteristics.rainLagNote: Tributaries add water fast after rain; High Gate can under-read a tributary-driven rise — cross-check Union.

localKnowledge: Normal flow @ High Gate — Dec–Feb 30–60 cfs; Mar–May 40–100; Jun–Nov 2–20; low flows 0.2–2; highest fishable 150–200 (Agnew).
Gradient 2.6 ft/mi. Seldom jetboatable even when high.
Public access scarce on the upper river — plan multi-day floats between accesses.

sections.name: Lower Bourbeuse (Noser Mill to the Meramec)
sections.description: The lower half from Noser Mill ~55 river-miles to the Meramec confluence. Flow stays inconsistent until Spring Creek (fed by Kratz Spring, ~25 cfs avg) enters ~10 mi below Noser Mill and can nearly double the river; from there it is nearly always floatable, though some riffles still walk in low water. A few impressive bluffs below Spring Creek; increasingly developed and murky near Union and below. Gradient 2.2 ft/mi.

putIn.name: Hwy 185 at Noser Mill (Devils Back Campground)

takeOut.name: Chouteau Claim MDC Access (mouth at the Meramec)

representativeGauge.rationale: Agnew keys the lower-river navigability table to the Bourbeuse River at Union gauge (Hwy 50 bridge)
representativeGauge.knownBias: reliable for the whole lower river except Noser Mill→Spring Creek, which runs lower than the gauge suggests

thresholds.source: Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview, lower-reach navigability table @ Union gauge: 'Less than 40 cfs—Most riffles will have to be walked.'
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '40-70 cfs—Most riffles will be runnable with considerable scraping bottom.'
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '70-120 cfs—low but floatable'; '120-250 cfs—easily floatable' → easily-floatable band begins at 120.
Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '120-250 cfs—easily floatable'; '250-500 cfs—high but floatable' → top of easily-floatable at 250.

Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: '250-500 cfs—high but floatable'; '500-1000 cfs—very high...strong currents and willow jungles may be dangerous.' (confidence HIGH: authoritative Agnew navigability table — owner-approved 2026-07-06.)

Al Agnew, OzarkAnglers Bourbeuse Overview: 'Over 1000 cfs—too high.' (500–1000 already flagged 'may be dangerous'; confidence HIGH: authoritative Agnew navigability table — owner-approved 2026-07-06.)

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: Below ~40 cfs at Union most riffles must be walked; 40–70 cfs runs with considerable scraping. The Noser Mill→Spring Creek stretch runs lower than the Union gauge reads. Jun–Nov normal (60–150 cfs) sits in the low-to-marginal range.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: 500–1000 cfs is very high and probably muddy, with strong currents and willow jungles that 'may be dangerous'; over 1000 cfs is too high. NWS UNNM7 flood/action stages auto-fill at ingest.

localKnowledge: Normal flow @ Union — Dec–Feb 150–250 cfs; Mar–May 300–500; Jun–Nov 60–150; low flows 30–60; highest fishable 600–1000 (Agnew).
Spring Creek (Kratz Spring, ~25 cfs avg) enters ~10 mi below Noser Mill and can nearly double the flow — below it the river is nearly always floatable.
Gradient 2.2 ft/mi. Below Union the river is incised into alluvial banks and typically very murky.

accessPoints.name: Mint Spring MDC Access
accessPoints.notes: Head of navigation. Upper reach mile 0.0.
accessPoints.description: MDC river access on the upper Bourbeuse in the northwest corner of Crawford County, about 9.5 miles south of Owensville via Route EE; effectively the head of navigation. A gravel ramp can take a vehicle and trailer, but the river here is shallow and best run by canoe.
accessPoints.roadAccess: From Owensville, Route EE south ~9.5 mi (NW Crawford County)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Lat/lon from MDC area-page embedded map data; effectively the head of navigation on the upper Bourbeuse.

accessPoints.name: Glaser Ford low-water bridge (Koenig Road)
accessPoints.description: Low-water bridge where Koenig Road crosses the upper Bourbeuse, known locally as Glaser Ford (Crawford County). Undeveloped roadside put-in; the concrete low-water slab can be overtopped and is hazardous at high flows.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) - Koenig Road x Bourbeuse River crossing (bridge=yes)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Genuine road-over-river crossing in OSM tagged bridge=yes, ~2.3 mi below Mint Spring; local name Glaser Ford, on Koenig Road. A separate 'Glaser Hollow Road' ford lies upstream of the head of navigation and is NOT this point.

accessPoints.name: Enke Road bridge
accessPoints.description: Bridge where Enke Road crosses the upper Bourbeuse. Roadside crossing only, with very limited/poor access.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) - Enke Road x Bourbeuse River crossing (bridge=yes)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: OSM road-over-river crossing, bridge=yes; longitude/mile consistent with position between Glaser Ford and Hog Trough.

accessPoints.name: Hog Trough Road low-water bridge
accessPoints.description: Low-water bridge carrying Hog Trough Road over the upper Bourbeuse. Slab crossing that can be overtopped at high water (hazard); undeveloped roadside access.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) - Hog Trough Road x Bourbeuse River crossing (bridge=yes)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: OSM road-over-river crossing, bridge=yes; low-water slab.

accessPoints.name: Tea MDC Access
accessPoints.description: MDC fishing and boat access on the upper Bourbeuse south of Owensville, Gasconade County. Provides a boat ramp to the river.
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Lat/lon from MDC area-page embedded map data (Gasconade County).

accessPoints.name: Mill Rock MDC Access
accessPoints.description: MDC access on the Bourbeuse in Franklin County (~10 acres), reached from Sullivan via Hwy 185, Route H and Mill Rock Road. Facilities include a parking lot and canoe launch; a scenic reach noted for bass and sunfish fishing.
accessPoints.roadAccess: From Sullivan, Hwy 185 north, Route H north ~10 mi, Mill Rock Road east ~3 mi
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Lat/lon from MDC area-page embedded map data (Franklin County).

accessPoints.name: Hwy CC bridge
accessPoints.description: MoDOT Route CC highway bridge over the Bourbeuse. Roadside crossing with very poor/limited access.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) - MoDOT Route CC x Bourbeuse River crossing
accessPoints.coordinateNote: OSM state-route x river crossing; longitude/mile consistent with position between Mill Rock and Wenkel Ford.

accessPoints.name: Wenkle Ford MDC Access
accessPoints.description: MDC access providing fishing and canoe access to the Bourbeuse near Spring Bluff, reached via Hwy 185 and Wenkel Ford Road. Gravel ford-type launch (OSM tags the river crossing ford=yes).
accessPoints.roadAccess: From Spring Bluff, Hwy 185 north, then Wenkel Ford Road north and west ~4 mi
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MDC area-page map data (MDC spells it 'Wenkel Ford Access'); an OSM ford=yes node at 38.32314,-91.25679 corroborates within ~100 m.

accessPoints.name: Laubinger Ford
accessPoints.notes: Upper mile 45.0. Very obscure and hard to find.
accessPoints.description: Very obscure, unimproved ford at the river end of Laubinger Ford Road (per OpenStreetMap), between Wenkel Ford and Peters Ford. Hard to find and without developed facilities.
accessPoints.roadAccess: River end of Laubinger Ford Road
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap - 'Laubinger Ford Road' river terminus (snapped to Bourbeuse channel)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Approximate: river point ~110 m from the mapped river-end of 'Laubinger Ford Road' in OSM. Very obscure/hard to find; verify on the ground.

accessPoints.name: Peters Ford
accessPoints.notes: Upper mile 53.0. Unimproved ford; parking non-existent without permission.
accessPoints.description: Unimproved low-water ford where Peters Ford Road meets the Bourbeuse, at the Peters Ford locality in Franklin County. No developed access.
accessPoints.roadAccess: End of Peters Ford Road (Peters Ford locality)
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) - Peters Ford Road x Bourbeuse River; corroborated by OSM 'Peters Ford' locality node (38.3745,-91.2076)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Road-over-river crossing of Peters Ford Road in OSM, matching the named Peters Ford locality.

accessPoints.name: Hwy 185 at Noser Mill / Devils Back Campground
accessPoints.notes: Upper mile 59.0 / lower mile 0.0. Bridge has no access; access below the mill dam (from the north) may be posted; safest is Devils Back Campground above the bridge (entails portaging Noser Mill dam).
accessPoints.description: Access on the Bourbeuse at Noser Mill, below the historic mill dam, where MoDOT Route 185 bridges the river (Franklin County). The adjacent Devil's Back Floats is a private, family-run campground and float outfitter operating here since 1980.
accessPoints.roadAccess: MoDOT Route 185 bridge at Noser Mill
accessPoints.facilities: Private riverside campground (Devil's Back Floats); public Hwy 185 bridge put-in below the old mill dam
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) - MO Route 185 x Bourbeuse River at Noser Mill; corroborated by 'Noser Mill' locality node (38.3931,-91.1924)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MO 185 bridge crossing at Noser Mill (put-in is just below the historic mill dam); marks upper mile 59.0 = lower mile 0.0.

accessPoints.name: Schmitt Ford
accessPoints.notes: Lower mile 4.2. Obscure, may be posted; end of Stuesse Road.
accessPoints.description: Obscure ford at the downstream end of Stuesse Road (per OpenStreetMap), Franklin County, just below Noser Mill. No developed facilities; hard to find.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap - 'Stuesse Road' downstream terminus (snapped to Bourbeuse channel)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Approximate: river point ~210 m from the terminus of Stuesse Road in OSM, matching the 'end of Stuesse Road' hint. river_mile is cumulative from head of navigation (Noser Mill 59.0 + lower 4.2).

accessPoints.name: Reiker Ford MDC Access
accessPoints.description: MDC access providing public fishing and boat access to the lower Bourbeuse (~10.9 acres), reached from Union via Hwy 50, Route UU, Snake Hill Road and Reiker Ford Road. Camping and target shooting are prohibited.
accessPoints.roadAccess: From Union, Hwy 50 west 1 mi, Route UU south 5 mi, Snake Hill Rd south 0.5 mi, Reiker Ford Rd east
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MDC area-page map data. river_mile is cumulative from head of navigation (Noser Mill 59.0 + lower 21.5).

accessPoints.name: Mayers Landing MDC Access
accessPoints.description: MDC access on the lower Bourbeuse (~6.6 acres), reached from Union via Hwy 50 west and Route UU south. The main channel has shifted away from the boat ramp, so direct channel access is limited.
accessPoints.roadAccess: From Union, Hwy 50 west 1 mi, then Route UU south 1 mi
accessPoints.facilities: Boat ramp (limited - channel has shifted away)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MDC area-page map data; MDC warns the main channel has shifted away from the boat ramp (limited access). river_mile cumulative = 59.0 + 32.5.

accessPoints.name: Union MDC Access
accessPoints.notes: Lower mile 40.9. Near the Hwy 50 / Union gauge.
accessPoints.description: MDC boat and fishing access on the Bourbeuse at the town of Union, off Hwy 50 near the Hwy 47 junction (~10.3 acres). Located near the USGS Union streamgage.
accessPoints.roadAccess: In Union off Hwy 50 near the Hwy 47 junction (adjacent to a QuikTrip)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MDC area-page map data; in the town of Union near the USGS Union streamgage. river_mile cumulative = 59.0 + 40.9.

accessPoints.name: Dr. Henry A. and Amalia Uhlemeyer MDC Access
accessPoints.description: MDC access (Dr. Henry A. and Amalia Uhlemeyer) providing fishing and boat access to the lower Bourbeuse in Franklin County.
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MDC area-page map data. river_mile cumulative = 59.0 + 49.9.

accessPoints.name: Chouteau Claim MDC Access
accessPoints.notes: Lower mile 55.2. At the mouth of the Bourbeuse (Meramec confluence).
accessPoints.description: MDC access (~15 acres) at the confluence of the Bourbeuse and Meramec rivers, reached from Moselle via St. Mary's Road. Largely undeveloped, with no boat ramp noted - primarily walk-in/canoe and bank fishing access.
accessPoints.roadAccess: From Moselle, St. Mary's Road northeast to the confluence
accessPoints.facilities: Undeveloped access at the Meramec confluence (no developed ramp noted)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: MDC area-page map data; at the Bourbeuse-Meramec confluence. river_mile cumulative = 59.0 + 55.2.

openQuestions: RIVER-TYPE [signoff]: Agnew explicitly confirms runoff-fed / murky / slowest-large-Ozark-stream character — 'spring_fed_float' mis-models the speed curve. Reclassify (a runoff/lowland float archetype, or 'flatwater'); not flipped unilaterally because riverType drives speed curves. isSpringFed already set false on both reaches.

DANGER ANCHORS [RESOLVED 2026-07-06]: high/dangerous cfs (upper 120/200 @ High Gate; lower 500/1000 @ Union) set to confidence 'high' on owner approval (Agnew's navigability table is authoritative). [safety] gate cleared. NWS UNNM7/HTGM7 flood stages remain an independent ft-based danger layer via auto-fetch.
Upper-reach gauge caveat: High Gate under-reads tributary-driven rises; consider surfacing the 'check Union after rain' rule in the app's upper-reach guidance.
Float times per reach not published by Agnew — derive from mileage + speed curve once river_type is settled.
